name: Basel-Landschaft (Switzerland)
description: A square white field charged with a red Baselstab (a stylised bishop's crosier) bearing seven crockets, the crook turned toward the fly.
history: Adopted in 1834 from the arms of Liestal. The red crosier recalls the bishops of Basel; the seven crockets and the staff turned to 'face away' from the city symbolise Basel-Landschaft's separation from Basel-Stadt.

features.role: red Baselstab with seven crockets, the crook turned toward the fly
features.symbolism: the bishops' crosier; separation from the city of Basel
